name: feature-loader-mutex-rejection
description: Phase 0 feature loader (ADR 0014) — verify the compatibility matrix rejects mutex pairs at setDisplayState time. Sets svoStorageBackend on, then attempts vdbBackend on — expects vdbBackend to remain off (rejection drops the offending portion of the partial). Also verifies invalidates is a soft warning (both toggles stay on, blocker emitted).

commands: (() => { const all = window.__canaryGetAllFeatureStatus(); if (all.svoStorageBackend?.mode !== 'on') throw new Error('B1 svoStorageBackend should be on, got ' + JSON.stringify(all.svoStorageBackend)); return 'B1 on'; })()

(() => { const all = window.__canaryGetAllFeatureStatus(); if (all.vdbBackend?.mode === 'on') throw new Error('B2 vdbBackend should have been rejected (mutex with B1), but it is on. all=' + JSON.stringify(all)); if (all.svoStorageBackend?.mode !== 'on') throw new Error('B1 should still be on after B2 rejection, got ' + JSON.stringify(all.svoStorageBackend)); return 'B2 mutex rejection verified'; })()

(() => { const all = window.__canaryGetAllFeatureStatus(); if (all.progressiveBrickBaking?.mode !== 'on') throw new Error('A3 should be on (soft invalidates is not a rejection), got ' + JSON.stringify(all.progressiveBrickBaking)); if (all.splatCache?.mode !== 'on') throw new Error('A6 should be on, got ' + JSON.stringify(all.splatCache)); const violations = window.__canaryValidateCurrentFeatures(); const soft = violations.filter(v => v.reason === 'invalidates'); if (soft.length === 0) throw new Error('expected at least one soft invalidates violation between A3 and A6, got ' + JSON.stringify(violations)); return 'soft invalidates verified: both toggles on, ' + soft.length + ' soft warning(s)'; })()
